assumptions: The question is asking about hedge accounting possibilities in consolidated financial statements for foreign exchange risk related to an intragroup dividend receivable.
The dividend receivable is being considered as either a recognised intragroup monetary item or as related to an intragroup transaction exposed to foreign exchange risk.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a recognised asset or liability as a possible hedged item, which makes fair value hedge accounting a distinct top-level hedge accounting model relevant to FX risk on a recognised receivable.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a forecast transaction as a possible hedged item, and specifically addresses highly probable forecast intragroup transactions for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model in consolidated financial statements for foreign exchange risk.
